title: Bill of Material (BOM)
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of a Bill of Material (BOM).
Learn different BOM types used in SAP.
Understand BOM usage in Production Planning and MRP.

introduction.title: What is a Bill of Material (BOM)?
introduction.content: A Bill of Material (BOM) is a structured list of all raw materials, semi-finished materials, assemblies, subassemblies and components required to manufacture a finished product. It acts as the recipe of a product and is one of the most important master data objects in SAP Production Planning.

business_purpose.title: Why is BOM Important?
business_purpose.content: SAP uses the BOM during Material Requirements Planning (MRP), Production Orders and Cost Estimation. Without an accurate BOM, SAP cannot calculate the correct material requirements for manufacturing.

bom_types.description: Defines components required for manufacturing a material.
Lists spare parts and components used for equipment maintenance.
Maintains components installed at a functional location.
Defines components sold together as one product.

bom_components.description: Defines the usage of the component.
Specifies the period for which the BOM is valid.

integration.purpose: Every BOM component must exist as a Material Master.
MRP explodes the BOM to calculate material requirements.
Production Orders automatically copy BOM components.
Calculates production cost based on BOM.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A stainless steel sink requires a steel sheet, welding wire, polishing compound and packaging material. These components are maintained in the BOM. When a Production Order is created, SAP automatically determines the required quantities of all components from the BOM.

best_practices: Keep BOM updated whenever engineering changes occur.
important_notes: Every component must have a valid Material Master.
A material can have multiple Alternative BOMs.
BOM directly affects MRP and Production Orders.
